name: Tafsir al-Mizan
author: Allamah Muhammad Husayn Tabatabai
text:
# Ayahs

```arabic
يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوٓا۟ إِذَا نَكَحْتُمُ ٱلْمُؤْمِنَـٰتِ ثُمَّ طَلَّقْتُمُوهُنَّ مِن قَبْلِ أَن تَمَسُّوهُنَّ فَمَا لَكُمْ عَلَيْهِنَّ مِنْ عِدَّةٍ تَعْتَدُّونَهَا ۖ فَمَتِّعُوهُنَّ وَسَرِّحُوهُنَّ سَرَاحًا جَمِيلًا (٤٩)
```

```english
O you who have faith! When you marry faithful women and then divorce them before you touch them, there shall be no period for you to reckon. But provide for them and release them in a graceful manner. (49)
```

```arabic
يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلنَّبِىُّ إِنَّآ أَحْلَلْنَا لَكَ أَزْوَٰجَكَ ٱلَّـٰتِىٓ ءَاتَيْتَ أُجُورَهُنَّ وَمَا مَلَكَتْ يَمِينُكَ مِمَّآ أَفَآءَ ٱللَّهُ عَلَيْكَ وَبَنَاتِ عَمِّكَ وَبَنَاتِ عَمَّـٰتِكَ وَبَنَاتِ خَالِكَ وَبَنَاتِ خَـٰلَـٰتِكَ ٱلَّـٰتِى هَاجَرْنَ مَعَكَ وَٱمْرَأَةً مُّؤْمِنَةً إِن وَهَبَتْ نَفْسَهَا لِلنَّبِىِّ إِنْ أَرَادَ ٱلنَّبِىُّ أَن يَسْتَنكِحَهَا خَالِصَةً لَّكَ مِن دُونِ ٱلْمُؤْمِنِينَ ۗ قَدْ عَلِمْنَا مَا فَرَضْنَا عَلَيْهِمْ فِىٓ أَزْوَٰجِهِمْ وَمَا مَلَكَتْ أَيْمَـٰنُهُمْ لِكَيْلَا يَكُونَ عَلَيْكَ حَرَجٌ ۗ وَكَانَ ٱللَّهُ غَفُورًا رَّحِيمًا (٥٠)
```

```english
O Prophet! Indeed We have made lawful to you your wives whom you have given their dowries, and those whom your right hand owns, of those whom Allah gave you as spoils of war, and the daughters of your paternal uncle, and the daughters of your paternal aunts, and the daughters of your maternal uncle, and the daughters of your maternal aunts who migrated with you, and a faithful woman if she offers herself to the Prophet and the Prophet desires to take her in marriage (a privilege exclusively for you, not for [the rest of] the faithful; We know what We have made lawful for them with respect to their wives and those whom their right hands own, so that there may be no blame on you ), and Allah is all-forgiving, all-merciful. (50)
```

```arabic
۞ تُرْجِى مَن تَشَآءُ مِنْهُنَّ وَتُـْٔوِىٓ إِلَيْكَ مَن تَشَآءُ ۖ وَمَنِ ٱبْتَغَيْتَ مِمَّنْ عَزَلْتَ فَلَا جُنَاحَ عَلَيْكَ ۚ ذَٰلِكَ أَدْنَىٰٓ أَن تَقَرَّ أَعْيُنُهُنَّ وَلَا يَحْزَنَّ وَيَرْضَيْنَ بِمَآ ءَاتَيْتَهُنَّ كُلُّهُنَّ ۚ وَٱللَّهُ يَعْلَمُ مَا فِى قُلُوبِكُمْ ۚ وَكَانَ ٱللَّهُ عَلِيمًا حَلِيمًا (٥١)
```

```english
You may put off whichever of them you wish and consort with whichever of them you wish, and there is no sin upon you [in receiving again] any [of them] whom you may seek [to consort with] from among those you have set aside [earlier]. That makes it likelier that they will be comforted and not feel unhappy, and all of them will be pleased with what you give them. Allah knows what is in your hearts, and Allah is all-knowing, all-forbearing. (51)
```

```arabic
لَّا يَحِلُّ لَكَ ٱلنِّسَآءُ مِنۢ بَعْدُ وَلَآ أَن تَبَدَّلَ بِهِنَّ مِنْ أَزْوَٰجٍ وَلَوْ أَعْجَبَكَ حُسْنُهُنَّ إِلَّا مَا مَلَكَتْ يَمِينُكَ ۗ وَكَانَ ٱللَّهُ عَلَىٰ كُلِّ شَىْءٍ رَّقِيبًا (٥٢)
```

```english
Beyond that, women are not lawful for you, nor that you should change them for other wives even though their beauty should impress you, except those whom your right hand owns. Allah is watchful over all things. (52)
```

```arabic
يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ لَا تَدْخُلُوا۟ بُيُوتَ ٱلنَّبِىِّ إِلَّآ أَن يُؤْذَنَ لَكُمْ إِلَىٰ طَعَامٍ غَيْرَ نَـٰظِرِينَ إِنَىٰهُ وَلَـٰكِنْ إِذَا دُعِيتُمْ فَٱدْخُلُوا۟ فَإِذَا طَعِمْتُمْ فَٱنتَشِرُوا۟ وَلَا مُسْتَـْٔنِسِينَ لِحَدِيثٍ ۚ إِنَّ ذَٰلِكُمْ كَانَ يُؤْذِى ٱلنَّبِىَّ فَيَسْتَحْىِۦ مِنكُمْ ۖ وَٱللَّهُ لَا يَسْتَحْىِۦ مِنَ ٱلْحَقِّ ۚ وَإِذَا سَأَلْتُمُوهُنَّ مَتَـٰعًا فَسْـَٔلُوهُنَّ مِن وَرَآءِ حِجَابٍ ۚ ذَٰلِكُمْ أَطْهَرُ لِقُلُوبِكُمْ وَقُلُوبِهِنَّ ۚ وَمَا كَانَ لَكُمْ أَن تُؤْذُوا۟ رَسُولَ ٱللَّهِ وَلَآ أَن تَنكِحُوٓا۟ أَزْوَٰجَهُۥ مِنۢ بَعْدِهِۦٓ أَبَدًا ۚ إِنَّ ذَٰلِكُمْ كَانَ عِندَ ٱللَّهِ عَظِيمًا (٥٣)
```

```english
O you who have faith! Do not enter the Prophet’s houses for a meal until you are granted permission, without hanging around for it to be readied. But enter when you are invited, and disperse when you have taken your meal, without cozying up for chats. Such conduct on your part offends the Prophet, and he is ashamed of [asking] you [to leave]; but Allah is not ashamed of [expressing] the truth. When you ask [his] womenfolk for something, do so from behind a curtain. That is more chaste for your hearts and theirs. You should not offend the Apostle of Allah, nor may you ever marry his wives after him. Indeed that would be a grave [sin] with Allah. (53)
```

```arabic
إِن تُبْدُوا۟ شَيْـًٔا أَوْ تُخْفُوهُ فَإِنَّ ٱللَّهَ كَانَ بِكُلِّ شَىْءٍ عَلِيمًا (٥٤)
```

```english
Whether you disclose anything or hide it, Allah indeed knows all things. (54)
```

```arabic
لَّا جُنَاحَ عَلَيْهِنَّ فِىٓ ءَابَآئِهِنَّ وَلَآ أَبْنَآئِهِنَّ وَلَآ إِخْوَٰنِهِنَّ وَلَآ أَبْنَآءِ إِخْوَٰنِهِنَّ وَلَآ أَبْنَآءِ أَخَوَٰتِهِنَّ وَلَا نِسَآئِهِنَّ وَلَا مَا مَلَكَتْ أَيْمَـٰنُهُنَّ ۗ وَٱتَّقِينَ ٱللَّهَ ۚ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ كَانَ عَلَىٰ كُلِّ شَىْءٍ شَهِيدًا (٥٥)
```

```english
There is no sin on them [in socializing freely] with their fathers, or their sons, or their brothers, or their brothers’ sons, or the sons of their sisters, or their own womenfolk, or what their right hands own. Be wary of Allah. Indeed Allah is witness to all things. (55)
```

```arabic
إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ وَمَلَـٰٓئِكَتَهُۥ يُصَلُّونَ عَلَى ٱلنَّبِىِّ ۚ يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ صَلُّوا۟ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلِّمُوا۟ تَسْلِيمًا (٥٦)
```

```english
Indeed Allah and His angels bless the Prophet; O you who have faith! Invoke blessings on him and invoke Peace upon him in a worthy manner. (56)
```

```arabic
إِنَّ ٱلَّذِينَ يُؤْذُونَ ٱللَّهَ وَرَسُولَهُۥ لَعَنَهُمُ ٱللَّهُ فِى ٱلدُّنْيَا وَٱلْـَٔاخِرَةِ وَأَعَدَّ لَهُمْ عَذَابًا مُّهِينًا (٥٧)
```

```english
Indeed those who offend Allah and His Apostle are cursed by Allah in the world and the Hereafter, and He has prepared a humiliating punishment for them. (57)
```

```arabic
وَٱلَّذِينَ يُؤْذُونَ ٱلْمُؤْمِنِينَ وَٱلْمُؤْمِنَـٰتِ بِغَيْرِ مَا ٱكْتَسَبُوا۟ فَقَدِ ٱحْتَمَلُوا۟ بُهْتَـٰنًا وَإِثْمًا مُّبِينًا (٥٨)
```

```english
Those who offend faithful men and women undeservedly, certainly bear the guilt of slander and flagrant sin. (58)
```

```arabic
يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلنَّبِىُّ قُل لِّأَزْوَٰجِكَ وَبَنَاتِكَ وَنِسَآءِ ٱلْمُؤْمِنِينَ يُدْنِينَ عَلَيْهِنَّ مِن جَلَـٰبِيبِهِنَّ ۚ ذَٰلِكَ أَدْنَىٰٓ أَن يُعْرَفْنَ فَلَا يُؤْذَيْنَ ۗ وَكَانَ ٱللَّهُ غَفُورًا رَّحِيمًا (٥٩)
```

```english
O Prophet! Tell your wives and your daughters and the women of the faithful to draw closely over themselves their chadors [when going out]. That makes it likely for them to be recognized and not be troubled, and Allah is all-forgiving, all-merciful. (59)
```

```arabic
۞ لَّئِن لَّمْ يَنتَهِ ٱلْمُنَـٰفِقُونَ وَٱلَّذِينَ فِى قُلُوبِهِم مَّرَضٌ وَٱلْمُرْجِفُونَ فِى ٱلْمَدِينَةِ لَنُغْرِيَنَّكَ بِهِمْ ثُمَّ لَا يُجَاوِرُونَكَ فِيهَآ إِلَّا قَلِيلًا (٦٠)
```

```english
If the hypocrites and those in whose hearts is a sickness, and the rumourmongers in the city do not desist, We will prompt you [to take action] against them; then they will not be your neighbours in it except briefly. (60)
```

```arabic
مَّلْعُونِينَ ۖ أَيْنَمَا ثُقِفُوٓا۟ أُخِذُوا۟ وَقُتِّلُوا۟ تَقْتِيلًا (٦١)
```

```english
Accursed, they will be seized wherever they are confronted and slain violently: (61)
```

```arabic
سُنَّةَ ٱللَّهِ فِى ٱلَّذِينَ خَلَوْا۟ مِن قَبْلُ ۖ وَلَن تَجِدَ لِسُنَّةِ ٱللَّهِ تَبْدِيلًا (٦٢)
```

```english
Allah’s precedent with those who passed away before, and you will never find any change in Allah’s precedent. (62)
```

## (Commentary)

The verses contain various rulings, some of which are specific to the Prophet (صلى‌الله‌عليه‌وآله) and his wives, while others are general.

**His saying, the Exalted**: **"O you who have faith! When you marry faithful women and then divorce them before you touch them, there shall be no period for them that you must reckon. So provide for them and release them in a graceful manner"** (33:49). What is meant by "marrying them" (نكاحهن) is the concluding of the marriage contract, and by "touching" (المس) is sexual intercourse (الدخول). "Providing for them" (التمتيع) means giving them an amount of wealth suitable to their status and circumstances, and "releasing them in a graceful manner" (التسريح بالجميل) means letting them go without dispute or harshness.

The meaning is: If you divorce women after the marriage contract but before intercourse, they are not required to observe a waiting period (العدة) — *the prescribed period a woman must wait after divorce or the death of her husband before she can remarry* — for that divorce, and it is obligatory to provide for them with some wealth and a graceful release.

The verse is absolute (مطلقة), covering both cases where a dower (المهر) has been specified and where it has not. It is thus restricted (يقيدها) by His saying: **"And if you divorce them before you have touched them, and you have already settled a dowry for them, then [pay them] half of what you have settled"** (2:237). Thus, the current verse remains the authoritative proof (حجة) for cases where no dower was specified.

**His saying, the Exalted**: **"O Prophet! Indeed We have made lawful for you your wives whom you have given their dowries"** (33:50) until the end of the verse. The Glorified mentions seven categories of women made lawful for His Prophet (صلى‌الله‌عليه‌وآله). The first category is in His saying: **"your wives whom you have given their dowries"**; what is meant by "rewards" (الأجور) here is the dowers (المهور). The second is in His saying: **"and those whom your right hand owns from what Allah has given you as spoils of war"** (ما ملكت يمينك مما أفاء الله عليك), meaning the female slaves he owns who came to him from the spoils (الغنائم) and the public wealth (الأنفال). The qualification of "what your right hand owns" as being "from what Allah has given you as spoils" is like the qualification of the wives by His saying "whom you have given their dowries"—it is for clarification (للتوضيح), not for exclusion (للاحتراز).

The third and fourth categories are in His saying: **"and the daughters of your paternal uncle and the daughters of your paternal aunts."** It is said this refers to the women of Quraysh. The fifth and sixth are in His saying: **"and the daughters of your maternal uncle and the daughters of your maternal aunts."** It is said this refers to the women of Banu Zuhrah. Regarding His saying: **"who emigrated with you,"** it is stated in *al-Majma‘*: "This was only the case before the lawfulness of non-emigrant women; then the condition of emigration for lawfulness was abrogated (نسخ)."

The seventh category is in His saying: **"and a faithful woman if she offers herself to the Prophet and the Prophet desires to take her in marriage."** This refers to a Muslim woman who offers herself to the Prophet (صلى‌الله‌عليه‌وآله), meaning she consents to marry him without a gift or dower (صداق ومهر); Allah made her lawful for him if he wished to marry her. His saying: **"specially for you, exclusive of the [rest of the] faithful"** is an announcement that this ruling—the lawfulness of a woman to a man through her offering herself—is among his specific privileges (خصائصه) and does not apply to the rest of the believers. His subsequent saying: **"We certainly know what We have made obligatory for them concerning their wives and those whom their right hands own"** is an affirmation of this ruling of exclusivity.

His saying: **"so that there may be no blame on you"** is a justification (تعليل) for His saying at the beginning of the verse: **"Indeed We have made lawful for you,"** or for the ruling of exclusivity at its end; the former is more apparent. The verse concludes with forgiveness and mercy.

**His saying, the Exalted**: **"You may put off whomever you wish of them, and take to yourself whomever you wish"** (33:51) etc. "Putting off" (الإرجاء) means delaying and distancing, which is an allusion (كناية) to rejection. "Taking to oneself" (الإيواء) means housing in a place, which is an allusion to acceptance and joining to oneself.

The context indicates that the meaning is that he (صلى‌الله‌عليه‌وآله) has the choice to accept or reject those who offer themselves to him.

His saying: **"and as for any you may seek of those you had set aside, there is no sin upon you."** "Seeking" (الابتغاء) is to request; meaning, those you seek from among those you had set aside and did not initially accept, there is no sin or blame upon you. That is, it is permissible for you to join to yourself those you had set aside and rejected from the women who offered themselves to you after the initial setting aside and rejection.

It is also possible that this is an indication that he (صلى‌الله‌عليه‌وآله) has the right to distribute his time among his wives or to waive the distribution (القسم). Thus, he may delay whomever he wishes and prioritize whomever he wishes, and set aside some from the distribution so he does not allot time to her, or seek her out and allot time to her after having set her aside. This is more in line with His subsequent saying: **"and as for any you may seek of those you had set aside, there is no sin upon you. That is more likely to delight their eyes"**—meaning they will be happy—**"and they will not grieve, and all of them will be pleased with what you give them. Allah knows what is in your hearts."** This is because the prioritized wife is happy with the time allotted to her, and the delayed wife hopes to be prioritized later.

His saying: **"And Allah is All-knowing, All-clement"** means He knows the best interests of His servants and does not hasten to punishment.

There are other various opinions regarding this verse, but what we have mentioned is most consistent with its occurrence in the context of the preceding verse and its connection to it, and this is what has been narrated from the Imams of the Ahl al-Bayt (عليه‌السلام), as will follow.

**His saying, the Exalted**: **"Beyond that, women are not lawful for you, nor that you should replace them with other wives, even though their beauty should attract you"** (33:52) etc. The apparent meaning of the verse, if assumed to be independent and not connected to what precedes it, is the prohibition of women for him (صلى‌الله‌عليه‌وآله) except for those who were given the choice and chose Allah; the denial of the permissibility of replacing them supports this.

However, if it is assumed to be connected to what precedes it—namely His saying: **"Indeed We have made lawful for you"** etc.—then its meaning is the prohibition of anyone other than those enumerated, which are the six categories mentioned previously.

In some narrations from some of the Imams of the Ahl al-Bayt (عليه‌السلام), it is stated that what is meant by the verse is the prohibited categories of women enumerated in His saying: **"Forbidden to you are your mothers, your daughters..."** (4:23).

Thus, His saying: **"Beyond that, women are not lawful for you"** means: after those who chose Allah and His Messenger, who were nine, according to the first meaning; or after those We enumerated in Our saying: "Indeed We have made lawful for you," according to the second meaning; or after the lawful ones, meaning the prohibited categories, according to the third meaning.

His saying: **"nor that you should replace them with other wives"** means to divorce some of them and marry others in their place. His saying: **"except those whom your right hand owns"** refers to female slaves, and it is an exception from His saying at the beginning of the verse: **"women are not lawful for you."**

His saying: **"And Allah is watchful over all things"** has an apparent meaning and contains a warning against opposition.

**His saying, the Exalted**: **"O you who have faith! Do not enter the Prophet’s houses unless permission is granted you"**—until His saying—**"from the truth"** (33:53). This is an explanation of the etiquette (أدب) of entering the houses of the Prophet (صلى‌الله‌عليه‌وآله). His saying: **"unless permission is granted you"** is an exception from the prohibition. His saying: **"for a meal"** is connected to the permission. His saying: **"without waiting for it to be readied"** (غَيْرَ ناظِرِينَ إِناهُ) means without waiting for the food to be prepared by entering early and staying a long time in anticipation of the meal. This is clarified by His saying: **"But when you are invited, enter, and when you have eaten, disperse."** His saying: **"without lingering for the sake of chat"** is coordinated with His saying: "without waiting for it to be readied," describing one state after another; meaning, do not stay while waiting for the food before the meal, nor while lingering for chat after the meal.

His saying: **"That used to hurt the Prophet, but he would be shy of [asking] you [to leave]"** is the justification for the prohibition. That is, do not stay like that because your staying used to cause the Prophet distress, yet he would be shy to ask you to leave. His saying: **"and Allah is not shy of the truth"** means He is not shy of clarifying the truth to you, which is mentioning his distress and teaching the appropriate etiquette.

**His saying, the Exalted**: **"And when you ask them for any object, ask them from behind a curtain. That is purer for your hearts and their hearts"** (33:53). The pronoun in "ask them" (سَأَلْتُمُوهُنَّ) refers to the wives of the Prophet (صلى‌الله‌عليه‌وآله). Asking them for an object is an allusion to speaking to them for a need; meaning, if a need arises for you to speak to the wives of the Prophet (صلى‌الله‌عليه‌وآله), then speak to them from behind a curtain (حجاب). His saying: **"That is purer for your hearts and their hearts"** is an explanation of the benefit of the ruling.

**His saying, the Exalted**: **"You should not hurt the Apostle of Allah, nor should you ever marry his wives after him"** (33:53) etc. Meaning, it is not for you to hurt him by opposing what you have been commanded regarding his wives or otherwise, nor are you ever to marry his wives after him. Indeed, that—your marrying his wives after him—is a grave matter with Allah. In the verse, there is an intimation that some of them mentioned something suggesting they would marry his wives after him, and that was indeed the case, as will come in the following discussion of narrations.

**His saying, the Exalted**: **"Whether you disclose anything or hide it, Allah indeed has knowledge of all things"** (33:54). Its meaning is apparent, and it is in reality a threatening reminder to whoever was hurting the Prophet (صلى‌الله‌عليه‌وآله) or mentioning the marriage of his wives after him.

**His saying, the Exalted**: **"There is no sin on them [if they appear] before their fathers"** (33:55) until the end of the verse. The pronoun in "on them" (عَلَيْهِنَّ) refers to the wives of the Prophet (صلى‌الله‌عليه‌وآله). The verse is in the sense of an exception from the general ruling of the veil (الحجاب). It has excepted fathers, sons, brothers, sons of brothers, and sons of sisters; these are the unmarriageable kin (المحارم). It is said that paternal and maternal uncles were not mentioned because they might describe the wives to their own sons.

It also excepted "their women" (نساءهن). The annexation of "women" to the pronoun "them" suggests that what is meant is the believing women, excluding the disbelieving ones, as previously mentioned regarding His saying: **"or their women"** (24:31). It also excepted those whom their right hands own, including both male and female slaves.

His saying: **"and be God-conscious of Allah. Indeed Allah is witness to all things"** contains an emphasis of the ruling, especially through the shift (التفات) from the third person to the second person in "be God-conscious of Allah" (وَاتَّقِينَ اللهَ).

**His saying, the Exalted**: **"Indeed Allah and His angels bless the Prophet. O you who have faith! Invoke blessings on him and invoke Peace upon him in worthy fashion"** (33:56). It has been mentioned previously that the root of *salat* (الصلاة) is "turning toward" (الانعطاف). Thus, His blessing, the Exalted, is His turning toward him with mercy—an absolute turning not restricted in the verse to one thing over another. Likewise, the blessing of the angels upon him is their turning toward him with sanctification and seeking forgiveness, and from the believers, it is the prayer for mercy.

In mentioning His blessing, the Exalted, and the blessing of His angels upon him before commanding the believers to bless him, there is an indication that the believers' blessing for him is an emulation of Allah, the Glorified, and His angels, and an emphasis of the subsequent prohibition.

Narrations have reached the level of being widely transmitted (استفاضت) through both Shi'i and Sunni chains that the way for the believers to bless him is to ask Allah, the Exalted, to bless him and his Progeny (آله).

**His saying, the Exalted**: **"Indeed those who offend Allah and His Apostle are cursed by Allah in the world and the Hereafter, and He has prepared for them a humiliating punishment"** (33:57). It is well known that Allah, the Glorified, is transcendent above being reached by offense or anything carrying the stain of deficiency or lowliness. Thus, mentioning Him with the Messenger and partnering Him in the offense against him is an honoring of the Messenger and an indication that whoever targets His Messenger with evil has also targeted Him with evil, for the Messenger, qua Messenger, has none but his Lord; so whoever targets him has targeted his Lord.

He has promised them the curse (اللعن) in this world and the Hereafter. The curse (اللعن) — *the removal and distancing from the mercy of Allah* — is the removal from mercy. The specific mercy for the believers is guidance to the true belief and the reality of faith (الإيمان), followed by righteous deeds (العمل الصالح). Thus, removal from mercy in this world is its prohibition upon them as a recompense for their deeds, which results in the sealing of hearts, as He said: **"We cursed them and made their hearts hard"** (5:13), and He said: **"but Allah has cursed them for their unfaith, so they do not believe, except a few"** (4:46), and He said: **"These are they whom Allah has cursed, so He made them deaf and blinded their sight"** (47:23).

As for the curse in the Hereafter, it is the removal from the mercy of proximity therein, as the Exalted said: **"No indeed! They will be barred from their Lord on that day"** (83:15).

Then He promised them that He has prepared for them—meaning in the Hereafter—a humiliating punishment. The punishment is described as "humiliating" (مهينا) because through their arrogance in this world, they intended to humiliate Allah and His Messenger, so they are met in the Hereafter with a punishment that humiliates them.

**His saying, the Exalted**: **"And those who offend faithful men and faithful women without their having committed any [fault], they certainly bear the guilt of calumny and a flagrant sin"** (33:58). The qualification of their offense as being "without their having committed any [fault]" is because offending them for what they have committed—as in retaliation (القصاص), prescribed punishments (الحد), and discretionary punishments (التعزير)—carries no sin.

As for offending them without cause and without desert, Allah considers it a bearing of calumny (البهتان) and flagrant sin. Calumny (البهتان) — *the act of falsely accusing someone to their face* — is lying about another to their face. The reason why an offense without cause is considered calumny is that the offender only offends due to a reason they consider a crime, saying: "Why did he say such-and-such?" or "Why did he do such-and-such?" when it is not a crime; thus, they calumniate them during the offense by attributing a crime to them to their face when it is not a crime.

It is a "flagrant sin" (إثماً مبيناً) because the intellect perceives slander and calumny to be sins without the need for a religious prohibition to be revealed.

**His saying, the Exalted**: **"O Prophet! Tell your wives and your daughters and the women of the faithful to draw their cloaks over themselves"** (33:59) etc. "Cloaks" (جلابيب) is the plural of *jilbab* (جلباب), which is a garment a woman wraps herself in so that it covers her entire body, or the head-covering (الخمار) with which she covers her head and face.

His saying: **"to draw their cloaks over themselves"** (يُدْنِينَ عَلَيْهِنَّ مِنْ جَلَابِيبِهِنَّ) means they should cover themselves with them so that their necklines and chests are not visible to onlookers.

His saying: **"That is more likely to distinguish them so that they are not harrassed"** means that covering the entire body is closer to them being recognized as people of modesty and righteousness, so they are not harassed; that is, the profligate will not harass them by accosting them.

It is also said the meaning is: that is closer to them being recognized as free Muslim women, so they are not accosted under the assumption that they are slaves or non-Muslim women from the People of the Book or others; the first meaning is more likely.

**His saying, the Exalted**: **"If the hypocrites and those in whose hearts is a sickness and the rumor-mongers in Madinah do not desist, We will surely rouse you against them"** (33:60) etc. "Desisting" (الانتهاء) from something is to refrain and cease from it. "Rumor-mongering" (الإرجاف) is the spreading of falsehood to cause grief and provoke agitation. "Rousing" (الإغراء) to an action is to incite toward it.

The meaning is: I swear, if the hypocrites and those in whose hearts is a sickness do not cease from corruption, and those who spread false news in Madinah to provoke agitation among the Muslims, We will surely incite you against them; then they will not be your neighbors in Madinah due to their expulsion from it, except for a little while—which is the time between the issuance of the command and its actual execution.

**His saying, the Exalted**: **"Accursed, they will be seized wherever they are confronted and slain a [ruthless] slaying"** (33:61). "Confronting" (الثقف) is to come upon something and overtake it. The phrase is a circumstantial qualifier (حال) for the hypocrites and those coordinated with them; meaning, in the state of being accursed, wherever they are found, they shall be seized and their killing shall be intensified until it encompasses them all.

**His saying, the Exalted**: **"Allah’s precedent with those who passed away before, and you will never find any change in Allah’s precedent"** (33:62). A "precedent" (السنة) is the established way that proceeds by its nature usually or always.

The Glorified says: This exemplary punishment with which We have threatened the hypocrites and those who follow their path—of expulsion and ruthless killing—is the precedent of Allah that proceeded among those of the past. Whenever a people went to extremes in corruption and provoking agitation among the people, and persisted and transgressed in that, We seized them likewise. You will never find any change in Allah’s precedent, so it shall proceed among you just as it proceeded among the nations before you.

## ( Discussion of Narrations )

In *al-Faqih*, ‘Amr ibn Shimr narrated from Jabir, from Abu Ja‘far (peace be upon him) regarding the saying of Allah, the Mighty and Majestic: **"and then divorce them before you touch them, there shall be no period for them that you must reckon. So provide for them and release them in a graceful manner"**; he said: "Provide for them (متعوهن), meaning give them gifts of what you are able from recognized kindness (معروف), for they return with sadness, loneliness, great anxiety, and the mockery of their enemies. Indeed, Allah is Generous, He is shy and loves the people of shyness; the most honorable of you is the one who is most honorable to his consorts."

In *al-Kafi*, with its chain to al-Halabi from Abu ‘Abdillah (peace be upon him) regarding a man who divorces his wife before he has entered upon her. He said: "He owes half the dower if he had specified something for her; and if he had not specified for her, then he must provide for her (فليمتعها) in the manner that women like her are provided for."

**I say**: The narrations with this meaning are numerous, and they are based on the specification (تخصيص) of this verse by the verse of *al-Baqarah*, as mentioned in the commentary of the verse.

In *al-Durr al-Manthur*, ‘Abd ibn Humayd extracted from Habib ibn Thabit who said: A man came to ‘Ali ibn al-Husayn and asked him about a man who said: "If I marry so-and-so, she is divorced." He said: "It is nothing; Allah began with marriage before divorce, for He said: **'O you who have faith! When you marry faithful women and then divorce them...'**"

**I say**: It was also narrated in *al-Majma‘* from Habib ibn Thabit from him (peace be upon him).

In the same source, Ibn Majah and Ibn Mardawayh extracted from al-Miswar ibn Makhramah from the Prophet (صلى‌الله‌عليه‌وآله) who said: **لا طلاق قبل نكاح ولا عتق قبل ملك** — "There is no divorce before marriage, and no manumission before ownership."

**I say**: The like of it was narrated from Jabir and ‘Aishah from him (صلى‌الله‌عليه‌وآله).

In *al-Kafi*, with its chain to al-Hadrami from Abu Ja‘far (peace be upon him) and with its chain to al-Halabi from Abu ‘Abdillah (peace be upon him) regarding the saying of Allah, the Mighty and Majestic: **"O Prophet! Indeed We have made lawful for you your wives"**; how many women were made lawful for him? He said: "Whatever he wished."

In the same source, with its chain to al-Halabi from Abu ‘Abdillah (peace be upon him) who said: I said: **"Beyond that, women are not lawful for you, nor that you should replace them with other wives"**? He said: "It was for the Messenger of Allah (صلى‌الله‌عليه‌وآله) to marry whomever he wished of the daughters of his paternal uncle and the daughters of his paternal aunts, and the daughters of his maternal uncle and the daughters of his maternal aunts, and his wives who emigrated with him. And it was made lawful for him to marry from the generality of the believers without a dower, which is the gift (الهبة); and the gift is not lawful except for the Messenger of Allah (صلى‌الله‌عليه‌وآله). As for anyone other than the Messenger of Allah, no marriage is valid except with a dower. That is the meaning of His saying, the Exalted: **'and a faithful woman if she offers herself to the Prophet'**."

In *al-Durr al-Manthur*, Ibn Sa‘d, Ibn Abi Shaybah, ‘Abd ibn Humayd, Ibn Jarir, Ibn al-Mundhir, and al-Tabarani extracted from ‘Ali ibn al-Husayn regarding His saying: **"and a faithful woman"**; she is Umm Sharik al-Azdiyyah who offered herself to the Prophet (صلى‌الله‌عليه‌وآله).

**I say**: It is also narrated that she was Khawlah bint al-Hakim, and that she was Layla bint al-Khatim, and that she was Maymunah. It appears that those who offered themselves were a number of women.

In *al-Kafi*, narrated from Muhammad ibn Qays from Abu Ja‘far (peace be upon him) who said: A woman from the Ansar came to the Messenger of Allah (صلى‌الله‌عليه‌وآله) and said: "O Messenger of Allah, a woman does not propose to a husband, and I am a widow with no husband for a long time, nor a child; do you have any need? If so, I have offered myself to you if you accept me." The Messenger of Allah spoke well to her and prayed for her. Then he said: "O sister of the Ansar, may Allah reward you well on behalf of the Messenger of Allah; your men supported me and your women desired me." Hafsah said to her: "How little is your shyness, how bold you are, and how intense is your desire for men!" The Messenger of Allah said: "Desist from her, O Hafsah, for she is better than you; she desired the Messenger of Allah while you blamed and faulted her." Then he said to the woman: "Depart, may Allah have mercy on you; Allah has made Paradise obligatory for you because of your desire for me and your seeking my love and pleasure. My command will reach you, if Allah wills." Then Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, revealed: **"and a faithful woman if she offers herself to the Prophet and the Prophet desires to take her in marriage, specially for you, exclusive of the [rest of the] faithful."** He said: "So Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, made lawful the woman's offering of herself to the Prophet (صلى‌الله‌عليه‌وآله), and that is not lawful for anyone else."

In *al-Majma‘*: It is said that when she offered herself to the Prophet (صلى‌الله‌عليه‌وآله), ‘Aishah said: "What is the matter with women that they give themselves without a dower?" Then the verse was revealed, and ‘Aishah said: "I see that Allah only hastens to fulfill your desire." The Messenger of Allah (صلى‌الله‌عليه‌وآله) said: "Indeed, if you obey Allah, He will hasten to fulfill your desire."

In *al-Majma‘* regarding His saying, the Exalted: **"You may put off whomever you wish of them, and take to yourself whomever you wish"**; Abu Ja‘far and Abu ‘Abdillah (peace be upon them) said: "Whoever he put off, he did not marry, and whoever he took in, he married."

In *al-Kafi*, with its chain to al-Hadrami from Abu Ja‘far (peace be upon him) regarding the saying of Allah, the Mighty and Majestic: **"Beyond that, women are not lawful for you"**; he said: "He only meant by it: the women whom Allah forbade to you in this verse: **'Forbidden to you are your mothers, your daughters, your sisters, your paternal aunts, your maternal aunts...'** until its end. If the matter were as they say, He would have made lawful for you what He did not make lawful for him, because any of you can replace [wives] whenever he wishes. But the matter is not as they say; indeed Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, made lawful for His Prophet (صلى‌الله‌عليه‌وآله) to marry of women whatever he wished, except what He forbade in this verse in Surah al-Nisa’."

In *al-Durr al-Manthur*, ‘Abd ibn Humayd, Ibn al-Mundhir, and Ibn Abi Hatim extracted through the path of ‘Ali ibn Zayd from al-Hasan regarding His saying: **"nor that you should replace them with other wives"**; he said: "Allah restricted him to his nine wives with whom he died." ‘Ali said: "So I informed ‘Ali ibn al-Husayn, and he said: 'Had he wished, he could have married others.'" And in the wording of ‘Abd ibn Humayd: "Rather, it was also for him to marry others."

In the *Tafsir* of al-Qummi: Regarding His saying, the Mighty and Majestic: **"O you who have faith! Do not enter the Prophet’s houses unless permission is granted you"**; when the Messenger of Allah (صلى‌الله‌عليه‌وآله) married Zaynab bint Jahsh—and he loved her—he gave a wedding feast and invited his companions. When his companions ate, they liked to converse in the presence of the Messenger of Allah (صلى‌الله‌عليه‌وآله), while he wished to be alone with Zaynab. So Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, revealed: **"O you who have faith! Do not enter the Prophet’s houses unless permission is granted you"**; that was because they used to enter without permission. So the Mighty and Majestic said: **"unless permission is granted you"** until His saying **"from behind a curtain."**

**I say**: The details of the story are narrated from Anas through various paths.

In *al-Durr al-Manthur*, Ibn Sa‘d extracted from Salih ibn Kaysan who said: "The veil (حجاب) of the Messenger of Allah was revealed over his wives in Dhu al-Qa‘dah in the fifth year of the Hijrah."

**I say**: It was also narrated by Ibn Sa‘d from Anas, and in it: "That year was the consummation of the Messenger of Allah (صلى‌الله‌عليه‌وآله) with Zaynab."

In the same source regarding His saying, the Exalted: **"You should not hurt..."** the verse: Ibn Abi Hatim extracted from al-Suddi who said: "It reached us that Talhah ibn ‘Ubaydillah said: 'Does Muhammad veil us from the daughters of our paternal uncles and then marry our women after us? If something happens to him, we shall surely marry his wives after him.' So the verse was revealed."

**I say**: Several narrations have been reported to this effect, and in some of them, it is mentioned that he intended ‘Aishah and Umm Salamah.

In *Thawab al-A‘mal*, from Abu al-Mu‘adh from Abu al-Hasan (peace be upon him) in a hadith, he said: I said: "What is the meaning of the blessing of Allah, the blessing of His angels, and the blessing of the believer?" He said: "The blessing of Allah is mercy from Allah, the blessing of the angels is sanctification (تزكية) from them for him, and the blessing of the believers is a prayer (دعاء) from them for him."

In *al-Khisal*, from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) in the Hadith of the Four Hundred, he said: **صلوا على محمد وآل محمد ـ فإن الله تعالى يقبل دعاءكم عند ذكر محمد** — "Invoke blessings upon Muhammad and the Progeny of Muhammad, for Allah, the Exalted, accepts your prayer upon the mention of Muhammad; and [He accepts] your prayer and your preservation of him when you recite: **'Indeed Allah and His angels bless the Prophet'**; so bless him whether you are in prayer or otherwise."

In *al-Durr al-Manthur*, ‘Abd al-Razzaq, Ibn Abi Shaybah, Ahmad, ‘Abd ibn Humayd, al-Bukhari, al-Muslim, Abu Dawud, al-Tirmidhi, al-Nasa’i, Ibn Majah, and Ibn Mardawayh extracted from Ka‘b ibn ‘Ujrah who said: A man said: "O Messenger of Allah, as for the peace (السلام) upon you, we have known it; but how is the blessing (الصلاة) upon you?" He said: "Say: O Allah, bless Muhammad and the Progeny of Muhammad, as You blessed Ibrahim and the Progeny of Ibrahim; indeed You are All-laudable, All-glorious. O Allah, prosper Muhammad and the Progeny of Muhammad, as You prospered Ibrahim and the Progeny of Ibrahim; indeed You are All-laudable, All-glorious."

**I say**: He cited eighteen ahadith other than this narration indicating the inclusion of the Progeny of the Prophet with him in the blessing, narrated by the authors of the *Sunan* and *Jawami‘* from several companions, including Ibn ‘Abbas, Talhah, Abu Sa‘id al-Khudri, Abu Hurayrah, Abu Mas‘ud al-Ansari, Buraydah, Ibn Mas‘ud, Ka‘b ibn ‘Ujrah, and ‘Ali (peace be upon him). As for the Shi'i narrations, they are beyond counting.

In the same source, Ahmad and al-Tirmidhi extracted from al-Hasan ibn ‘Ali that the Messenger of Allah (صلى‌الله‌عليه‌وآله) said: **البخيل من ذكرت عنده فلم يصل علي** — "The miser is he in whose presence I am mentioned and he does not invoke blessings upon me."

In the *Tafsir* of al-Qummi regarding His saying, the Exalted: **"O Prophet! Tell your wives and your daughters and the women of the faithful to draw their cloaks over themselves"**; the occasion of its revelation was that women used to go out to the mosque and pray behind the Messenger of Allah (صلى‌الله‌عليه‌وآله). When it was night and they went out for the Maghrib and ‘Isha’ prayers, young men would sit in their path and harass them and accost them. So Allah revealed: **"O Prophet!"** the verse.

In *al-Durr al-Manthur*, ‘Abd al-Razzaq, ‘Abd ibn Humayd, Abu Dawud, Ibn al-Mundhir, Ibn Abi Hatim, and Ibn Mardawayh extracted from Umm Salamah who said: "When this verse was revealed: **'to draw their cloaks over themselves'**, the women of the Ansar went out as if there were crows upon their heads from the black garments they wore."

In the *Tafsir* of al-Qummi regarding His saying, the Exalted: **"If the hypocrites... do not desist"**; it was revealed concerning a group of hypocrites who were in Madinah, spreading rumors (يرجفون) about the Messenger of Allah (صلى‌الله‌عليه‌وآله) when he went out on some of his expeditions, saying: "He has been killed and captured," causing the Muslims to grieve. They complained to the Messenger of Allah (صلى‌الله‌عليه‌وآله), so Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, revealed: **"If... do not desist"** until His saying **"except for a little while"**; meaning, We shall command you to expel them from Madinah except for a little while.

**"Accursed, they will be seized wherever they are confronted and slain a [ruthless] slaying."** In the narration of Abu al-Jarud from Abu Ja‘far (peace be upon him), he said: "**Accursed** (ملعونين); thus the curse became mandatory upon them, curse after curse, by the word of Allah."
